commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Fear. Thus they abuse the patience of God, and grow worse, because he is good. His time will come, Apocalypse xvi. 15., Ecclesiasticus v. 4., and 2 Peter iii. 10.

commentaries.author: John Cassian
commentaries.content: Up until now, when you never gainsaid him either by your own or by anybody else’s response, you gave him leave to have the mastery in you, as Solomon says: “Because those who do evil are not quickly opposed, therefore the heart of the children of men is full within them, so that they may do evil.” And therefore, after he has been disclosed, this most wicked spirit will no longer be able to disturb you, nor shall the filthy serpent ever again seize a place to make his lair in you, now that by a salutary confession he has been drawn out from the darkness of your heart into the light. .

commentaries.author: John Cassian
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Because sentence against an evil work is not executed speedily, therefore the heart of the sons of men is fully set in them to do evil. It is clear, then, that no one can be deceived by the devil except the person who has chosen to offer him the assent of his will. Ecclesiastes has expressed this plainly in these words: “Because those who do evil are not quickly opposed, therefore the heart of the children of men is full within them, so that they may do evil. ” Hence it is evident that a person transgresses because, when wicked thoughts attack him, he does not at once resist and oppose them.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
